title: Aeschynomene triflora
intro: Aeschynomene triflora, also known as the trifoliate horse bean, is a plant species in the legume family, Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate congestion and soothe irritated tissues. The bioactive constituents of Aeschynomene triflora include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Aeschynomene triflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or bean family. It belongs to the tribe Dalbergieae and the subtribe Dalbergiinae. Within the genus Aeschynomene, Aeschynomene triflora is one of the many species found. The classification of Aeschynomene triflora is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other related species. Aeschynomene triflora is a legume native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world.

In some cultures, the plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, Aeschynomene triflora has been used to treat fever and other minor health issues. parts: Aeschynomene triflora, also known as the "three-flower horse bean" or "trifloral horse bean", is a plant native to tropical Africa. Aeschynomene triflora, its leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
